title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: The over priced liberal arts and private universities are the schools that gave huge scholarships. They ended being the lowest cost. So if you're a top student DO apply to those!
author: @anniek4919

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: Back in 2004 at age 24 University of Phoenix got me for three classes before I realized I could get the same education at a community college for a fraction of the price.
author: @malcorub

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: Correction to the private vs public schools point: private colleges often offer generous financial aid that can make it cheaper to attend than a state school. State schools offer offer almost no institutional aid since they don't have the large endowments of private and are already offering such a reduced tuition to begin with. If you are interested in a non profit accredited private college then go ahead and apply and see how much fin aid they offer you and what the conditions to receive it are. Don't write off private colleges as "too expensive" just by looking at the sticker price of cost of attendance because almost nobody besides the super rich actually pay that amount.
author: @first9299

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: I agree with everything Shane is saying. There are really some schools out there that are focused more on earning rather than to educate and give value to what they can equip their graduates with.
author: @fordrobertson3461

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: My daughter applied to 6 or 7 colleges and universities.   I assumed the state schools would be cheaper. I was incorrect.   The private college gave my daughter a competitive scholarship based on her top 5% achievements in high school.   Still insanely expensive but the college has a solid internship program which I believe is very important.
author: @jjs777fzr

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: I went to a private college and I am PROUD I did. I got a full-tuition scholarship to go there and I had the best experience ever!

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: I think we can just agree that the best course of action is a community College transfer to state university for us who do not have absurd amounts of money saved

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: I would say this is my 2nd favorite Shane video after the "Useless degrees" one. lol No sugarcoating just straight up facts about these colleges. We need to completely avoid them!
author: @thespiderexterminator2683

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: The point about finding a college that supports you is spot on.  Not just for finding a career afterward, but guiding you through your academics for the time you're attending.  Ask current students; how is your academic advisor?  Are they proactive in catching problems before they get too bad?  Regular scheduled meetings during the school year?  Is there tutoring and essay writing assistance located in the library?  Some schools just leave you to fend for yourself; they graduate students who swim and discard those who sink.
author: @bridgecross

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: How about a video of “online colleges” how good are they , what the benefits and negatives . The cost of online vs in person , any recommendations if any for online colleges , just more information on online colleges or a online college vs traditional colleges video …. Thanks really find your videos helpful and definitely been doing some of the things you talk about
author: @josegutierrez9703

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: Community colleges need to stop saying they are "2-year" colleges. Judging by the progam length, the difficulty of some of the classes, and so forth, 2 ends up turning into 3-5. (4-6 depending on the situation)
author: @coffeeismyvice

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: I'm 26 years old and will be attending a private university in Austin, Texas this fall semester! (I'm really excited after returning back to my home city from new student orientation). I avoided college until mainly because I lacked the structure from not understanding what I'd like to do as a career. Honestly I wasn't aware of the way private universities work (or at least the school where I'm a student at). I just avoided private schools due to the cost. It blows my mind about how opposite my personal case worked out! Summary: 50,000 a year. I received federal grants, 3 school grants, then applied for the 2 small loans my fafsa could offer. My total per semester as a full time on campus student with meal plan is five thousand a semester. That's it. I can more than work with only 10K a year if it means I get to be a part of that campus I've already found my belonging with. My lack of research could have made this happen years earlier ago for me, but once admissions got into contact with me I've gained many big new insights. That is how I found this video because I'm so interesting in learning more about this new concept in my life lol
author: @BrookePainter

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: Shane, I was wondering if you’re able to make a video on scholarships? I would like to try living on campus for one semester but I cannot afford to pay for it. I’m a sophomore in college (in STEM, CSE), in honors and I have a high GPA. Thanks!!

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: Shane, does the bit about for profit schools include ALL non profit schools, or can there be ones that provide a decent education that an employer wouldn’t just throw out?
author: @Potus_313

title: Colleges you need to avoid...
body: my cousin got into NYU and Brown but it was so expensive. So instead he chose Kings College London, UK which ranked 35th globally and cost 70% less. If anyone is interested in going to a good University i would suggest not just looking in USA but also Europe. The major advantage is that the undergraduate degree is just 3 years and someone can get masters degree as well for far less cost.
author: @greatindiandreams

title: Lafayette College - 5 Things I Wish I Knew Before Attending
body: Lafayette was Greek dominated when I went there 25 years ago. That's probably the case at many small liberal arts colleges with a Greek system.  At least Easton is MUCH nicer now than it was back then. No one went downtown back in the day except to Mothers.  Also, do activities - I was on the Activities Forum and we got to meet cool bands like the Gin Blossoms and the Samples. And I was a prison tutor (that was interesting) and volunteered in the soup kitchen.
author: @michellerosenfeld8333

title: Lafayette College - 5 Things I Wish I Knew Before Attending
body: I graduated from Lafayette College in 1989. Wonderful small, second tier college. Great opportunity to participate in college athletics and springboard for obtaining graduate degrees. Very expensive. Smaller than many high schools in Pennsylvania. Options include: Colgate, F & M, Bucknell, Allegheny, Hamilton, Colby, Union. Very similar options in second tier schools. Proximity to Philadelphia and NYC was fantastic. Beach in the summer in NJ.
author: @courtneycaplin6773

title: Lafayette College - 5 Things I Wish I Knew Before Attending
body: The Greek life I have a problem with. The living in a dorm I find okay. The narrator said that it deprives you of having a real world experience of living. Listen sweetie, you will spend THE REST OF YOUR LIFE having real world living experience! What's the rush? Plus, you are safer in a dorm than apartment.
author: @wendyharper8245

title: 2021 Lafayette College Freshman Move-In Day ft. Renee!
body: Hi guys! I hope you'll all love waking up to my highly anticipated college vlog! I've been through hell and back trying to release this thing so I hope it lives up to the hype :D (also apparently an end screen has to go at the end of the video smh, I'll figure this out some day)
author: @chlickflicks

title: 2021 Lafayette College Freshman Move-In Day ft. Renee!
body: Wow, this brings back some memories. I moved into my Freshman dorm room in the basement of South College way back in August, 1982. I don't think my roommate and I put up any decorations at all, and I'm also pretty sure we didn't clean the room once the entire year. Your sister and her roommate did it right...
author: @suchanhachan

title: Lafayette College Campus Tour
body: That fraternity house looks so well built, and massive, as to be one of the main buildings of the College itself...!
author: @JohnGLewis1964

title: Lafayette College Campus Tour
body: @JohnGLewis1964 Layafette College had interesting architecture. What variety too! I made a separate video on the Fraternity House. It was designed by a Lafayette College graduate. I was lucky to get to see the inside.
author: @wworldp

title: Lafayette College Campus Tour
body:
Thank you again, Wanda, that was fun! 
Yes, that Fraternity you pictured there is quite the building!  If I recall accurately, it has a large porch on the back of the structure (which you were picturing) with very pleasant views.  I am a Lehigh graduate, and I would say, even so, that that particular frat beats (slightly) any of ours....
And I have always loved that entrance to the town/campus (the lone stone arch) they have at the bottom of the hill too...
author: @JohnGLewis1964

title: Lafayette College Drone Campus Tour 2021
body: The memories that I have retained from my time at Lafayette are some of my best and most interesting stories. I was a member of DU in the late 90s early 2000s. Beer Pong and Grain punch forever!
author: @rustyforceps1012
